title: Classification of Signals
summary: This page discusses properties of periodic signals, including that the integral over any interval of one period duration is the same regardless of starting point. It also introduces the concepts of everlasting signals, causal signals, noncausal signals, and anti-causal signals, with definitions and examples referencing earlier figures.

figures.caption: Generation of a periodic signal by periodic extension of its segment of one-period duration.
figures.description: The figure has two time-domain plots, labeled (a) and (b), with horizontal axis t and vertical axis x(t). Each plot shows a piecewise linear waveform consisting of a flat top followed by a downward ramp to zero, repeated by shifting one highlighted one-period segment along the time axis; marked time points include -7, -1, 0, 2, 5, 11 in (a) and -6, 0, 6, 12 in (b). The shaded regions indicate one-period segments, and students should notice how copying this segment at equal time intervals generates the full periodic signal.
